slow.title: Beginner Typist
slow.desc: You are typing with visual search and hunt-and-peck. With proper hand posture and touch typing practice, you can easily double this score!

average.title: Average Typist
average.desc: This is the global average typing speed. You can handle basic office tasks, but learning touch typing could save you dozens of hours every month.

aboveAverage.title: Productive Typist
aboveAverage.desc: You are faster than 70% of people! You can comfortably write emails, reports, and code without feeling held back by your fingers.

pro.title: Professional Typist
pro.desc: Outstanding! You are faster than 95% of people. This speed is typical for professional programmers, journalists, and transcriptionists.

god.title: Typing Grandmaster
god.desc: Phenomenal! You are in the top 1% of typists globally. Your typing speed matches the speed of thought. You are a typing machine!

howItWorks.accuracyDesc: Raw speed is nothing without control. An error-filled paragraph slows down comprehension. The net WPM score subtracts uncorrected errors to ensure real-world capability.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subheading: Got questions about typing speed tests, multiplayer races, or custom builders? We've got answers.

items.q: What is a good typing speed?
items.a: For most professional jobs (like writers, programmers, or office admins), a good typing speed is between 50 to 80 WPM. Anything above 80 WPM is considered highly fluent, while speeds over 100 WPM are elite.

items.q: How can I increase my WPM?
items.a: The best way to increase your typing speed is by learning touch typing. This means using all ten fingers and keeping your hands on the home row without looking down. Focus on typing accurately, and speed will follow naturally.

items.q: Can I build my own custom typing test?
items.a: Yes! With our Custom Typing Test Builder, you can create fully personalized typing exercises. You can paste your own text, upload text files, or generate custom themed paragraphs using our integrated DeepSeek AI coach.

items.q: How does the Multiplayer Typing Race work?
items.a: Our real-time multiplayer mode allows you to create or join typing rooms. You can copy the invite link to challenge friends, or race other active typists online. Live leaderboards show progress bars and WPM scores instantly.

items.q: Are there interactive games to improve typing speed?
items.a: Yes! In the Games section, you can play HTML5 typing games like 'Zombie Word Defense' (Z-Type) and 'Falling Words'. These games help you build muscle memory and finger reaction speed in an engaging way.

items.q: What keyboard layouts and languages are supported?
items.a: We support standard layouts including QWERTY, Dvorak, and Colemak, as well as multiple languages. You can customize keyboard layout settings directly inside the typing config bar.

Yes, accuracy is vital. While 'Raw WPM' measures how fast you physically hit keys, 'Net WPM' deducts penalties for mistakes. Practicing with high accuracy prevents you from spending extra time pressing backspace to correct errors.

items.q: How is WPM different from CPM?
items.a: WPM stands for Words Per Minute, which counts a 'word' as 5 characters. CPM stands for Characters Per Minute, which measures the exact number of individual keystrokes (letters, numbers, spaces, and punctuation) you type in one minute.

items.q: Are typing tests free on this platform?
items.a: Yes! All typing tests, lessons, multiplayer modes, arcade games, and analytics charts on englishtypingtest.org are 100% free. You can practice without even creating an account, though an account helps track your historical stats.
